steps.title: Recognize that both equations represent the same line
steps.content: Check if the second equation is a multiple of the first. Dividing $12x + 21y = 33$ by 3 gives $4x + 7y = 11$, which is identical to the first equation. Since both equations represent the same line, any point on this line satisfies both equations, and there are infinitely many solutions.

steps.title: Parameterize the line with x = r
steps.content:
For any real number $r$, if $x = r$, then $y = -\frac{4r}{7} + \frac{11}{7}$, giving the point $(r, -\frac{4r}{7} + \frac{11}{7})$.

Verify by substituting into the first equation: $4r + 7\left(-\frac{4r}{7} + \frac{11}{7}\right) = 4r - 4r + 11 = 11$. That matches the computed answer, so A is correct.
